title: विवाह पंचमी स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विवाह पंचमी स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ सीताराम विवाह स्तुति ॥

सीतारामौ सदा पूज्यौ दाम्पत्यस्य शिरोमणी।
विवाहपञ्चम्यां युक्तौ वन्दे तौ जानकीपतिम्॥

श्रीरामं दशरथसूनुं जानकीवल्लभं विभुम्।
शिवधनुर्भञ्जनं वीरं वन्दे विवाहमण्डपे॥१॥

जनकनन्दिनी सीता रामवामाङ्गशोभिनी।
वरमाला गृहीत्वा सा रामं वरयते स्वयम्॥२॥

मार्गशीर्षे शुक्लपक्षे पञ्चम्यां पुण्यतिथौ शुभे।
सीतारामविवाहोऽभूत् धन्या सा तिथिरुत्तमा॥३॥

जनकराजसभायां शिवधनुर्भञ्जनम्।
सीतायाः स्वयंवरे रामं विजयन्तं नमाम्यहम्॥४॥

वैदेहीं विमलां सीतां रामं दाशरथिं परम्।
दाम्पत्यधर्ममाश्रित्य पूजयामि निरन्तरम्॥५॥

सीताया हृदये रामो रामस्य हृदये च सा।
अभिन्नौ तौ यथा सूर्यप्रभायोरन्तरं न हि॥६॥

वरस्य लक्षणं रामो वधूत्वे जानकी शुभा।
एतयोर्दाम्पत्यमेव धर्मस्य मूलकारणम्॥७॥

जयति जयति रामो जानकीप्राणनाथो
जयति जयति सीता रामवामाङ्गवासा।
जयति जयति लक्ष्मीर्भरतः शत्रुघ्नश्च
जयति जयति विप्रा वेदपारायणस्थाः॥८॥

विवाहमण्डपे दिव्ये वेदघोषपुरस्कृते।
सीतारामविवाहस्य स्मृतिर्मे सुखदा सदा॥९॥

ॐ सीतारामाभ्यां नमः।
दाम्पत्यसुखमाधत्स्व सन्तानं च सुखं बहु।
सीतारामविवाहस्य स्मरणं सर्वमङ्गलम्॥१०॥

॥ इति श्री विवाह पंचमी स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Vivāha Pañchamī Stotram ||

|| Sītārāma Vivāha Stuti ||

Sītārāmau sadā pūjyau dāmpatyasya śiromaṇī.
Vivāhapañchamyāṁ yuktau vande tau Jānakīpatim.

ŚrīRāmaṁ Daśarathasūnuṁ Jānakīvallabhaṁ vibhum.
Śivadhanurbhañjanaṁ vīraṁ vande vivāhamaṇḍape.||1||

Janakannandinī Sītā Rāmavāmāṅgaśobhinī.
Varamālā gṛhītvā sā Rāmaṁ varayate svayam.||2||

Mārgaśīrṣe śuklapakṣe pañchamyāṁ puṇyatithau śubhe.
Sītārāmavivāho'bhūt dhanyā sā tithiruttamā.||3||

Janakarājasabhāyāṁ Śivadhanurbhañjanam.
Sītāyāḥ svayaṁvare Rāmaṁ vijayantaṁ namāmyaham.||4||

Vaidehīṁ vimalāṁ Sītāṁ Rāmaṁ Dāśarathiṁ param.
Dāmpatyadharmamāśritya pūjayāmi nirantaram.||5||

Sītāyā hṛdaye Rāmo Rāmasya hṛdaye cha sā.
Abhinnau tau yathā sūryaprabhāyorantaraṁ na hi.||6||

Varasya lakṣaṇaṁ Rāmo vadhūtve Jānakī śubhā.
Etayordāmpatyameva dharmasya mūlakāraṇam.||7||

Jayati jayati Rāmo Jānakīprāṇanātho
Jayati jayati Sītā Rāmavāmāṅgavāsā.
Jayati jayati Lakṣmīrbharataḥ Śatrughnaścha
Jayati jayati viprā Vedapārāyaṇasthāḥ.||8||

Vivāhamaṇḍape divye vedaghoṣapuraskṛte.
Sītārāmavivāhasya smṛtirme sukhadā sadā.||9||

Om Sītārāmābhyāṁ Namaḥ.
Dāmpatyasukhamādhatsva santānaṁ cha sukhaṁ bahu.
Sītārāmavivāhasya smaraṇaṁ sarvamaṅgalam.||10||

|| Iti Shrī Vivāha Pañchamī Stotraṁ sampūrṇam ||
hindiMeaning: (1) दशरथ पुत्र श्रीराम, जानकीवल्लभ, शिवधनुष भंजन करने वाले वीर को विवाह मण्डप में वन्दन। (2) जनकनन्दिनी सीता, राम के वामांग को शोभित करने वाली, वरमाला लेकर स्वयं राम को वरती हैं। (3) मार्गशीर्ष शुक्लपक्ष पंचमी की पुण्य शुभ तिथि को सीताराम का विवाह हुआ, धन्य है वह उत्तम तिथि। (4) जनक राजसभा में शिवधनुष भंजन करके सीता के स्वयंवर में विजयी राम को नमस्कार। (5) विमल वैदेही सीता और परम दाशरथि राम के दाम्पत्य धर्म को आश्रय कर निरन्तर पूजा करता हूँ। (6) सीता के हृदय में राम और राम के हृदय में सीता - जैसे सूर्य और प्रभा में अन्तर नहीं, वैसे वे अभिन्न हैं। (7) आदर्श वर राम और आदर्श वधू जानकी - इनका दाम्पत्य ही धर्म का मूल कारण है। (8) जय हो राम की, सीता की, लक्ष्मी की, भरत-शत्रुघ्न की, वेदपारायण करने वाले विप्रों की। (10) सीताराम को नमस्कार, दाम्पत्य सुख, सन्तान और बहुत सुख प्रदान करें। सीताराम विवाह का स्मरण सर्वमंगल है।
benefits: Celebrates the divine wedding of Sita and Rama on Vivaha Panchami
Invokes ideal marital harmony modeled on sita-rama's relationship
Invokes the blessing of marital happiness, progeny, and family prosperity
Remembrance of this divine wedding is Sarva Mangala (all-auspicious)
Ideal for recitation during wedding ceremonies and anniversaries
Specifically powerful on Margashirsha Shukla Panchami
description: Vivaha Panchami Stotram — विवाह पंचमी स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Lord Rama. Attributed to Ramayana Tradition / Puranic Compilation, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to remembrance of this divine wedding is Sarva Mangala (all-auspicious) and celebrate the divine wedding of Sita and Rama on Vivaha Panchami. It is also said to grant ideal marital harmony modeled on Sita-Rama's relationship. Recitation is particularly auspicious during Vivah Panchami and Ram Navami. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 6 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Vivaha Panchami Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Vivaha Panchami Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Sacred prayer celebrating Sita-Rama's divine wedding for marital bliss and harmony.

seoFaqs.question: What is Vivaha Panchami and what does this stotram celebrate?
seoFaqs.answer: Vivaha Panchami is the annual commemoration of the divine wedding of Sri Rama and Sita on Margashirsha Shukla Panchami (the fifth lunar day of the bright half of Margashirsha, falling in November or December). The stotram celebrates this sacred marriage as the dharmic ideal for all Hindu unions and as the cosmic union of Vishnu (Rama) with Lakshmi (Sita). The festival is observed with greatest grandeur at Janakpur Dham (Sita's birthplace, in present-day Nepal) and at Ayodhya, Rama's birthplace, with re-enacted weddings, processions, and special temple rituals. Verse 3 of the stotram explicitly names the date and verse 4 the Janaka-sabha venue.

seoFaqs.question: What is the source of the Vivaha Panchami Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The Vivaha Panchami Stotram is preserved in the Ramayana tradition and the Padma Purana's Patala Khanda which contains extensive narratives of Sita-Rama's wedding. The composition draws on episodes from Valmiki Ramayana Bala Kanda chapters 66 to 73, the Adhyatma Ramayana, the Anand Ramayana, and Tulsidas's Ramcharitmanas Bala Kanda. Several similar Vivah-stutis exist in regional traditions; this version focuses on the dharmic-sublime aspects of the wedding and is widely recited at Janakpur, Ayodhya and across Vaishnava households on Margashirsha Shukla Panchami and during family wedding ceremonies as a mangala-prarthana.

seoFaqs.question: Why was Sita-Rama's wedding held on Margashirsha Shukla Panchami?
seoFaqs.answer: After King Dasharatha's family arrived at Mithila in response to Janaka's invitation, the muhurta calculated by court astrologers fixed the wedding on Margashirsha Shukla Panchami as the most auspicious of the available dates following Rama's victory at the Sita-swayamvara. Verse 3 of the stotram celebrates this as dhanya sa tithir-uttama, blessed is that supreme tithi. Astrologically, Margashirsha (also called Agrahayana) was the original first month of the ancient Vedic calendar (Krishna himself declares in the Gita masanam Margashirsho-aham, among months I am Margashirsha), and the Shukla Panchami within it carries Lakshmi-tithi shri-yoga benefits.

seoFaqs.question: Why are weddings traditionally avoided on Vivaha Panchami in some North Indian regions?
seoFaqs.answer: A widespread folk tradition in parts of Mithila, Awadh and northern India avoids fixing one's own family wedding on Vivaha Panchami despite its auspiciousness, with the reasoning that Sita-Rama's marriage, though divinely blessed, was followed by 14 years of Vanavasa (forest exile) for the couple. Householders therefore prefer to celebrate Vivaha Panchami as a remembrance and seek blessings from it, but choose other muhurtas for their own children's weddings. This is, however, a regional convention only; many South Indian, Janakpur and Ayodhya traditions actively celebrate Vivaha Panchami with mass-marriages, treating it as the most auspicious day for new unions.

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Vivaha Panchami Stotram bestow?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram is widely recited for marital harmony, prayer for marriage of unmarried sons and daughters (especially of those facing prolonged delay), restoration of harmony in troubled marriages, blessings of progeny (verse 10 explicitly invokes santanam cha sukham bahu, progeny and abundant happiness), and the general mangala (auspiciousness) of all aspects of family life. Verse 6's image of Sita in Rama's heart and Rama in Sita's heart, inseparable as the sun and its radiance, is the meditative ideal for couples seeking spiritual union. The closing line declares that mere remembrance of Sita-Rama's wedding is sarva-mangalam, all-auspicious.

seoFaqs.question: How is Vivaha Panchami celebrated at Janakpur and Ayodhya?
seoFaqs.answer: At Janakpur Dham in Nepal, the Janaki Mandir (Sita's birthplace) hosts a multi-day celebration including a barat (wedding procession) symbolically arriving from Ayodhya with the Rama murti, formal kanyadaan ceremonies with priests reciting this and other Vivah stotras, mass community feasts, and a pradakshina of the temple. At Ayodhya, the Vivah celebration is held at temples including the new Ram Mandir and the historic Kanak Bhawan (the palace gifted by Kaikeyi to Sita as a wedding present, where Sita-Rama murtis are worshipped as bride and groom). Devotees from across India undertake the Janakpur-Ayodhya parikrama specifically during Vivaha Panchami week.
